optimist.name: Fiona Delgado-Chen
optimist.column:
The Giants lost again, but here’s the thing: they’re still brewing something special. You can’t measure a team’s potential by a single game, especially when the calendar says even years and the magic feels like it’s been put on hold. Torture baseball, my favorite. That’s what we’re getting here—late-inning drama, one-run losses, and the kind of grit that turns into rings down the road.  

The 6-10 record is a reminder that the NL West is a beast, but let’s not confuse the scoreboard with the story. This team has

pessimist.name: Bud Petrowski
pessimist.column:
The Giants lost again. Not to a team with a legitimate shot at the playoffs, but to the Orioles, a team that’s probably still figuring out how to dress for winter. Two runs. Six. A record that’s now 6-10, a half-season in the books, and the Giants still looking like a team that’s been handed a playbook written in reverse. Not Giants baseball.  

You know what’s worse than losing?
